title: Palindromic Substrings
description:
Given a string `s`, return the number of palindromic substrings in it.

A string is a palindrome when it reads the same backward as forward.

A substring is a contiguous sequence of characters within the string.

 

Example 1:

Input: s = "abc"
Output: 3
Explanation: Three palindromic strings: "a", "b", "c".

Example 2:

Input: s = "aaa"
Output: 6
Explanation: Six palindromic strings: "a", "a", "a", "aa", "aa", "aaa".

hint_plan.level_1: How can we reuse a previously computed palindrome to compute a larger palindrome?
hint_plan.level_2: If “aba” is a palindrome, is “xabax” a palindrome? Similarly is “xabay” a palindrome?
hint_plan.level_3:
Complexity based hint:
If we use brute force and check whether for every start and end position a substring is a palindrome we have O(n^2) start - end pairs and O(n) palindromic checks. Can we reduce the time for palindromic checks to O(1) by reusing some previous computation?
hint_plan.level_4:
Complexity based hint:
If we use brute force and check whether for every start and end position a substring is a palindrome we have O(n^2) start - end pairs and O(n) palindromic checks. Can we reduce the time for palindromic checks to O(1) by reusing some previous computation?

canonical_solution_summary:
Complexity based hint:
If we use brute force and check whether for every start and end position a substring is a palindrome we have O(n^2) start - end pairs and O(n) palindromic checks. Can we reduce the time for palindromic checks to O(1) by reusing some previous computation?
solution_sentences: while left >= 0 and right < n and s[left] == s[right]:
hints_per_sentence: Start by defining the function with the given name and parameter.
Calculate the length of the string to use for bounds in loops.
Initialize a variable to keep track of the total number of palindromic substrings found.
Loop over all possible centers for palindromes, considering both odd and even lengths.
Compute the left index for the current center based on integer division.
Compute the right index by adjusting for even-length palindromes using modulo.
Expand outward from the center while the indices are within bounds and characters match.
Increment the count each time a valid palindrome is found during expansion.
Move the left index one step to the left to continue checking a longer palindrome.
Move the right index one step to the right to match the left movement.
After checking all centers, return the total count.
final_explanation: This solution uses a center expansion approach to efficiently count palindromic substrings. By iterating through each possible center (including positions between characters for even-length palindromes) and expanding outward while characters match, it avoids redundant checks and runs in O(n^2) time with O(1) extra space. This method leverages the symmetry of palindromes to count all substrings without explicitly generating each one.
